**Acceptance Criteria**

Fail. The shared helper exists at [crates/jit/src/labels.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/labels.rs:216), and the originally listed sites now call it, for example [commands/issue.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/commands/issue.rs:36), [commands/batch_create.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/commands/batch_create.rs:553), [commands/breakdown.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/commands/breakdown.rs:607), and [commands/validate.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs:838). The helper has behavior tests at [labels.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/labels.rs:477).

Blocking issue: `type:` label semantics are still scattered, so the “single shared helper is the only place that knows the `type:` label encoding” criterion is not met.

- [type_hierarchy.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/type_hierarchy.rs:591) still checks type membership by comparing labels to `format!("type:{}", t)` instead of using the shared type-label extractor.
- [domain/queries.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/domain/queries.rs:210) still queries strategic issues via `matches_pattern(..., &format!("type:{}", type_value))`.
- [validation/rules.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs:367) still builds `format!("type:{ty}")` and compares raw labels directly.
- [commands/validate.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs:190) still performs type replacement by constructing full `type:*` strings directly.

**Architecture / Separation**

No storage, command, CLI, or output boundary regression found. The blocker is domain/validation/command code retaining parallel type-label encoding knowledge instead of delegating to the shared label primitive.

**Functional Paradigm**

The new helper code is pure and iterator-based. The remaining issue is not imperative style; it is duplicated label semantics.

**Correctness / Invariants**

No new issue found for atomic writes, event logging, gate semantics, assignee format, label validation, PID safety, or unsafe code in this change surface. The centralization invariant for `type:` label handling remains violated by the sites above.

**Testing / Gates**

Used the provided succeeded cargo-ci gate for current HEAD `a12c200b49e3df838499520c81e06147c452920b`: `.jit/gate-runs/ce3a0335-6509-40d4-bee4-997133760563/result.json` reports `fmt: ok`, `clippy: ok`, and `test: 2720 passed, 0 failed, 12 ignored`.

I did not rerun cargo tests. I ran `./scripts/jit-validate.sh`; it passed.

**Error Handling**

No new `unwrap()` / `expect()` in non-test library code found in the reviewed change surface, and no error-context regression identified.

**CLI Contract**

No CLI command surface change found; no `--json` regression identified.

**Documentation**

The new public label APIs have doc comments with examples at [labels.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/labels.rs:122). Documentation is not the blocker.

**Prior Review Feedback**

The most recent failed review’s specific items were partially addressed: `type_hierarchy::extract_type` now delegates to label helpers, `domain/projection.rs` now uses `type_label_value`, and `validation/graph.rs` no longer calls `values_in_namespace(..., TYPE_NAMESPACE)`. The broader acceptance issue remains because other non-test paths still reconstruct or match `type:*` labels directly.

**Jit Issue Management**

`./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed. `issue.dependencies` is empty, so there are no prerequisite issues to validate.

VERDICT: FAIL
